commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an early no-op return to the private browser profile `setDeep` helper when `keys` is empty and then writes using the final key directly.

PR surface: Source +1. Total +1 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. at the private-helper source level: current main would write `obj[""]` if `setDeep` received an empty key array. There is no high-confidence user-flow reproduction because current callers pass non-empty literal arrays.

target.title: fix: guard setDeep against empty keys array in Chrome profile decoration
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where `setDeep()` with empty `keys` would create a property with empty-string key on the target object.

## Why This Change Was Made

Add early `if (keys.length === 0) return;` guard. Production callers (`decorateOpenClawProfile`) pass non-empty literal arrays like `["profile","info_cache","Default","name"]`.

## User Impact

No user-visible change. Defensive guard against edge cases.

## Evidence

```
extensions/browser/src/browser/profiles.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  36 passed (36)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 16.96s
```
target.author: zhanglei99586
